source: Unauthenticated GET requests with attacker-selected distinct deviceId values.
closest_control: ProfileService.read creates a durable row on every miss with no creation budget, rate control, or expiry.
sink: MariaDB user_profile and its unique index receive one row per distinct identifier.
impact: Sustained requests can exhaust storage/write/index capacity and degrade shared profile availability.
preconditions: Profile API is reachable.
Many distinct values fit the 36-character contract.
No external throttle blocks sustained requests.
counterevidence: One bounded row is created per request.
Length/uniqueness bound row shape, not count.
Deployment rate limits or quotas may exist but are not evidenced.
proof_gaps: Gateway limits, firewall exposure, database quotas, and practical exhaustion rate are unknown.

evidence.claim: Public GET accepts caller-selected deviceId.
A read miss saves a new profile.
Each distinct device_id is durable and uniquely indexed.

affected_locations.detail: Unauthenticated GET /api/v1/profiles/{deviceId} accepts an arbitrary path selector and invokes read.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: read delegates to transactional getOrCreate, which saves a UserProfile whenever the caller-selected deviceId is absent.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The repository lookup/save path has no requester rate, creation budget, or existence-only read alternative.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Each accepted distinct value becomes a durable unique user_profile row; IDs of up to the column's 36-character capacity are plentiful.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: Public mutation routes.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Miss saves a new profile.
affected_locations.label: allocation

affected_locations.detail: GET of any path-selected deviceId calls the profile read service.
affected_locations.label: public_entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: read delegates to getOrCreate and saves a new entity on every unique miss.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Each attacker-chosen identifier becomes a durable entity key.
affected_locations.label: allocation_sink

affected_locations.detail: user_profile stores every unique device_id durably; VARCHAR(36) still permits unlimited unique values.
affected_locations.label: database_sink

fix_direction: Make read misses non-mutating or issue server IDs, and enforce public creation budgets/rate controls.
rationale: The public write-on-read primitive permits unbounded durable allocation; deployment controls bound practical exploitation.

source: Unauthenticated consent and allergy-create requests against a selected profile.
closest_control: No per-profile allergy count exists; each distinct value is appended and the full collection remapped.
sink: MariaDB allergy rows grow and ProfileResponse serializes the complete list.
impact: Repeated additions can consume storage and create growing read/mutation work, degrading profile availability.
preconditions: Caller enables consent.
Caller submits many distinct valid names.
No external request budget intervenes.
counterevidence: Fields are length-bounded and exact duplicate raw names are unique.
One request adds one row.
Normal UI use is short.
proof_gaps: External admission controls and practical exhaustion threshold are unknown.

evidence.claim: Public consent and allergy creation are callable.
Each call appends/flushes without count limit.
Every response maps the full allergy collection.

affected_locations.detail: An anonymous caller can opt any selected profile in and then repeatedly POST validated allergy records.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Every call appends, flushes, and maps the entire profile allergy list; no duplicate or maximum count check exists.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The unbounded List accepts every AllergyIngredient and cascades persistence.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Each mutation/read serializes all accumulated allergy rows, making cumulative repeated-add response work grow quadratically.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The entity has no per-profile cardinality constraint; V1 only prevents repeating the same raw ingredient name, so distinct values remain unbounded.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

fix_direction: Enforce a clinically justified maximum, bounded responses, and deployment admission controls.
rationale: Per-request validation does not prevent durable distinct-child growth or response amplification.

One request inserts one row.

evidence.claim: Public favorite creation accepts selected deviceId.
Service appends/flushes without count maximum.

affected_locations.detail: Each add flushes a new row and maps the full profile/favorites list before filtering back to one response; updates scan and remap the full list.

affected_locations.detail: Profile reads materialize and return every favorite. Fields are individually bounded, but the collection is not paged or capped.

source: Concurrent opt-out and allergy-create requests for one initially opted-in deviceId.
closest_control: Separate stale transactions lack row locks, optimistic versioning, and a database consent/child constraint.
sink: An allergy insert may commit after opt-out clears its snapshot; reads do not gate rows on consent.
impact: Health data may remain stored and disclosed after consent withdrawal.
preconditions: Consent begins true.
The add transaction reads true before opt-out commits.
Opt-out commits before the add transaction inserts and flushes its child row.
counterevidence: Sequential opt-out deletes rows.
Add rejects consent observed as false.
Exploitation requires concurrent requests for the same profile.

evidence.claim: Opt-out and add are independent transactions with a one-time consent check.
Entity has no version and enforces consent only inside one persistence context.
Stored allergy rows serialize without a consent gate.

affected_locations.detail: Consent update and allergy creation are separate concurrently callable anonymous HTTP operations.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Each method opens an independent ordinary transaction, loads its own profile snapshot, and neither locks nor rechecks consent at commit.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: findByDeviceId has no pessimistic lock and the entity has no optimistic @Version field.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Opt-out clears the first transaction's collection, while a stale true snapshot in another transaction can still attach a new child.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Subsequent reads serialize allergy rows without gating the list on rememberAllergies, exposing the retained row alongside false consent.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Independent consent/add methods.
affected_locations.label: transactions

affected_locations.detail: No @Version or DB-enforced relation.
affected_locations.label: missing serialization

affected_locations.detail: The bearer-profile consent update and allergy create actions are independently reachable public requests and can run concurrently for one deviceId.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Separate transactions load the same profile. addAllergy checks the stale in-memory consent value once, then inserts and flushes; updateConsent clears only the collection visible to its own transaction.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: The aggregate has no optimistic version, and its consent setter and allergy insertion enforce the invariant only within one persistence context.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The database stores the consent flag and allergy rows independently; no constraint or trigger prevents an allergy insert when remember_allergies is false.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Serialize consent/allergy mutation and enforce the invariant at commit/database and read boundaries.
rationale: A deterministic two-transaction schedule leaves a post-withdrawal allergy row or restores stale consent; either outcome violates §2-5, so runtime reproduction is a regression test rather than missing proof.

source: Concurrent country-update and consent opt-out requests after both load consent enabled.
closest_control: The same non-versioned entity has no locking or @DynamicUpdate, so default ORM SQL may write a stale consent column.
sink: A later country update may persist remember_allergies=true after opt-out.
impact: Withdrawn health-data consent may be silently restored, enabling later retention.
preconditions: Both transactions load consent enabled before opt-out commits.
Opt-out commits before the country update flushes.
The later country update uses the target's pinned default non-dynamic Hibernate update path.
counterevidence: updateCountry does not explicitly change consent.
The race requires concurrent requests for the same profile.
Versioning, locking, or @DynamicUpdate would prevent this exact stale write, but none is configured.

evidence.claim: Country and consent changes are independent transactions.
Fields share a non-versioned entity without @DynamicUpdate.
Database stores consent without intent/locking constraint.

affected_locations.detail: PATCH profile country and PATCH profile consent are independently callable for the same bearer-like deviceId.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Each operation opens its own transaction and loads the same UserProfile without serialization, version checking, or a commit-time consent recheck.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: findByDeviceId is an ordinary non-locking lookup; there is no locked profile query.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: countryCode and rememberAllergies share one non-versioned, non-@DynamicUpdate parent entity; opt-out clears children but a stale country entity still holds rememberAllergies=true.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: The persisted consent bit is the opt-in authority; no database constraint or trigger prevents a later stale parent update from restoring true.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Add versioning or serialization and intent-specific updates that cannot stale-write consent.
rationale: Spring Boot 3.5.16 pins Hibernate 6.6.53.Final; for this non-versioned entity without @DynamicUpdate, the normal update path binds all updateable properties, so the later country update deterministically writes stale consent=true after opt-out.

source: Allergy rows created under V1 before consent existed.
closest_control: V2 sets existing profiles to consent false without deleting, quarantining, or reconciling existing child rows.
sink: ProfileResponse maps every hydrated allergy row despite false consent.
impact: After migration, legacy health data remains stored and disclosed while memory is reported disabled.
preconditions: A real database upgrades from V1 with allergy rows.
V2 runs unchanged.
Profile is read.
counterevidence: Fresh installs have no legacy rows.
Runtime opt-out clears rows, but Flyway does not invoke it.
Bearer remains required for profile read.
proof_gaps: Whether any deployment contains pre-V2 allergy rows is unknown.

evidence.claim: V1 permits allergy rows without a consent column.
V2 defaults false and leaves children untouched.
Response unconditionally serializes loaded allergy rows.

affected_locations.detail: V2 defaults every existing profile to remember_allergies=FALSE but performs no DELETE, consent backfill, quarantine, or data migration for existing children.

affected_locations.detail: Allergies exist without consent.
affected_locations.label: old schema

affected_locations.detail: False default, no data reconciliation.
affected_locations.label: migration

affected_locations.detail: Serializes all rows.
affected_locations.label: disclosure

affected_locations.detail: The migration assigns FALSE to every pre-existing profile but performs no delete or consent-preserving data migration for existing allergy rows. Lines are identical to immutable source blob 96c28d131d81cbc35780cc066a598245af7ab485.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The public bearer-deviceId profile read returns ProfileService.read without an additional consent filter.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper

affected_locations.detail: ProfileResponse.from serializes every p.getAllergies() row even when p.remembersAllergies() is false.

affected_locations.detail: The normal API withdrawal path clears the collection with orphanRemoval, but Flyway's direct column addition never invokes this setter.

rationale: For any upgraded database with legacy rows, retention/disclosure under false consent is deterministic.

source: A developer running the local ERD helper after overriding the MariaDB root password in .env.
closest_control: The helper passes the root password as a literal docker client argument instead of stdin or a protected option source.
sink: A permitted same-host process observer can inspect the short-lived docker client argv.
impact: The local MariaDB root credential may be disclosed to another local principal.
preconditions: A developer runs bin/gen-erd.py.
The root password is non-public and another local principal can inspect process argv at the right moment.
That principal lacks an equivalent path to the same credential.
counterevidence: This is a manual local-development helper, not a network entrypoint.
Exposure is brief and many Macs are single-user.
Same-user malware or Docker-daemon access generally grants equivalent capability.
The default example root password is already public.
proof_gaps: Actual multi-user macOS argv visibility and workstation policy were not tested.

evidence.claim: The helper reads MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD and passes -pPASSWORD to docker exec.
The value is the local MariaDB root credential and may be overridden.

affected_locations.detail: The script reads MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD from .env into a process-global string.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: Every metadata query launches docker exec with '-p' + PW as a literal command-line argument.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The value is the database root credential, not a restricted read-only metadata account.
affected_locations.label: configuration_source

affected_locations.detail: Password concatenated into process argv.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The script reads MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD from the repository .env into the global PW value.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Each metadata query invokes docker exec with the literal argument -p plus PW, exposing the secret in the docker client command-line argument vector.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Pass the password through a protected option file/stdin or container environment without placing it in host argv.
rationale: Deterministic local secret exposure survives, but the narrow same-host/manual preconditions keep severity low.

source: A network peer able to reach a developer host running the default Compose MariaDB service.
closest_control: Short-form port publishing exposes 3306 beyond loopback while setup copies predictable credentials without forcing replacement.
sink: The persistent application MariaDB accepts SQL using public fallback credentials.
impact: A reachable peer can read/alter allergy profiles and saved locations or destroy service availability.
preconditions: Developer runs Compose defaults.
Host port 3306 is reachable through Docker/firewall/network configuration.
Default credentials remain unchanged.
counterevidence: The stack is explicitly local-development infrastructure.
Firewall, Docker Desktop, or network isolation may block peers.
Credentials are overrideable and the template advises replacement.
proof_gaps: No live socket, firewall, LAN reachability, or retained credential was tested.

evidence.claim: MariaDB publishes 3306 using public fallback credentials and no loopback host address.
Setup copies the public environment template.
Template contains predictable database credentials.
Schema includes profiles, allergies, and favorites.

affected_locations.detail: MariaDB receives public fallback credentials and publishes container port 3306 without a loopback-only host address.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The documented normal setup path copies the public environment template before Compose is started.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: The copied template supplies the publicly known application password change-me-locally and does not force replacement.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: The application schema contains anonymous device ids, opt-in allergy ingredients, saved facilities, and free-text memos.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Bind development MariaDB to 127.0.0.1 and require generated non-default credentials.
rationale: The weak listener is concrete if reachable; deployment reachability remains explicit uncertainty.

source: A network peer able to reach default Compose Redis on port 6379.
closest_control: Redis runs without authentication/ACL and is published beyond loopback; cached values lack writer integrity.
sink: Unauthenticated commands can read/delete/replace caches consumed as government-derived drug and facility facts.
impact: A reachable peer can flush caches, exhaust resources, cause denial of service, and potentially inject serializer-compatible false facts.
preconditions: Compose Redis is running and reachable.
Application consumes the same instance.
Semantic poisoning additionally requires valid keys and serializer-compatible payloads.
counterevidence: Stack is documented for local development.
Firewall or network isolation can block reachability.
Serializer restricts classes and no RCE gadget is shown.
Cache TTL is six hours.
proof_gaps: Live socket/firewall exposure and a valid semantic poison payload were not tested.

evidence.claim: Redis is published without auth or loopback-only binding.
Caches accept allowed polymorphic types without authenticity.
Predictable DUR cache values feed official contraindication output.

affected_locations.detail: Redis is started without an ACL/password configuration and published on host port 6379 without a loopback-only host address.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Six-hour Redis values use polymorphic JSON restricted to application and Java utility types; this blocks arbitrary classes but provides no cache writer authentication or integrity check.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Government-published drug contraindication warnings are read from a predictable durWarnings cache.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Pharmacy rows and LIVE/FIXTURE provenance are read from a predictable coordinate-keyed cache and can influence rendered facility facts.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Keep Redis loopback-only; authenticate shared instances and strictly type/authenticate cached values.
rationale: Unauthenticated control is direct if reachable; deployment and typed-payload uncertainty are preserved.

source: The same MariaDB and Redis listeners represented by R01-CAN-130 and R01-CAN-131.
closest_control: No shared control exists beyond two independent service configurations.
sink: The same database and cache sinks covered by the child findings.
impact: No distinct combined impact remains after the two children are fixed.
preconditions: At least one already-covered development listener is reachable.
counterevidence: Database and cache use different protocols, controls, impacts, and fixes.
Nested-worktree portions are absent.

evidence.claim: MariaDB half is fully covered by R01-CAN-130.
Redis half is fully covered by R01-CAN-131.

affected_locations.detail: The application database user defaults to mermaid/mermaid and port 3306 is published without a loopback host address.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The remotely reachable database operates on the persistent mariadb-data volume.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Redis is launched with the image default command, no authentication or access-control configuration, and a short-form all-address port publication.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Unauthenticated Redis commands operate on a persistent redis-data volume used for application cache state.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Track the two service-specific findings, not a duplicate composite.
rationale: The umbrella adds no independent source-control-sink tuple.

source: The candidate names only absent nested-worktree Redis/cache/drug files.
closest_control: Immutable-target existence is the controlling validation check.
sink: No claimed sink exists in the target.
impact: No target-resident impact exists for this instance.
preconditions: The absent nested paths would have to exist.
counterevidence: Every affected_location is absent from the immutable tree.
Outer Redis exposure and cache integrity remain covered by R01-CAN-131.

affected_locations.detail: The stock Redis image is published on host port 6379 with no password, ACL, or loopback-only bind in the Compose definition.
affected_locations.label: exposed_data_store_boundary

affected_locations.detail: Spring config supplies only Redis host and port and uses Redis for six-hour public-API caches.
affected_locations.label: missing_client_authentication

affected_locations.detail: DefaultTyping.EVERYTHING plus the com.mermaid/java collection allowlist accepts serializer-compatible expected application records from Redis; there is no value authenticity check.
affected_locations.label: deserialization_sink

affected_locations.detail: Attacker-known cache names/keys return allowed Permitted and PermittedDetail record shapes that carry product, ingredient, licence, and prescription facts.
affected_locations.label: concrete_cache_consumer

affected_locations.detail: Cached records feed allergy checks and medicine responses and receive server-authored MFDS provenance.
affected_locations.label: trusted_medical_sink

fix_direction: No target change for this instance; use the outer-root candidate.
rationale: All claimed affected locations are absent.

source: Malformed same-version data in this browser tab's own sessionStorage.
closest_control: The generic reader omits runtime shape validation for chat-session data.
sink: ChatProvider dereferences null at startup without an error boundary.
impact: The user can blank their own local app until storage is cleared; no independent remote or cross-user impact is established.
preconditions: Same-origin script, extension, local tools, or corrupt legacy state writes null.
The same tab starts the app.
counterevidence: sessionStorage is same-origin and tab-scoped.
An XSS/extension actor can already disrupt/read the app directly.
Current writer does not produce null.
proof_gaps: No legacy release known to write same-version null was identified.

evidence.claim: Version match returns unchecked data and chat load has no validator.
Initialization dereferences session.messages.
No root error boundary contains the crash.

affected_locations.detail: A matching schemaVersion returns parsed.data unchanged whenever the caller omits isValid; the TypeScript cast adds no runtime validation.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_parser

affected_locations.detail: loadChatSession supplies no validator, so {schemaVersion:'1.0',data:null} returns null instead of EMPTY_SESSION and remains in sessionStorage.
affected_locations.label: missing_chat_validator

affected_locations.detail: ChatProvider passes the null value to restoreSession, whose session.messages dereference throws before the provider or shell renders.
affected_locations.label: dereference_sink

affected_locations.detail: The root has no error boundary or recovery path, so the startup exception removes the full application safety surface.
affected_locations.label: missing_containment

affected_locations.detail: read() validates JSON syntax and schemaVersion but returns parsed.data without checking that it exists or has the requested runtime shape.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_source

affected_locations.detail: loadChatSession returns that unchecked value from sessionStorage as ChatSession.
affected_locations.label: concrete_loader

affected_locations.detail: restoreSession dereferences session.messages during ChatProvider initialization; a null/undefined session fails before its Array.isArray recovery branch can run.
affected_locations.label: crash_sink

affected_locations.detail: The root mounts MobileShell directly with no error boundary around ChatProvider initialization.
affected_locations.label: uncontained_application_root

fix_direction: Add runtime validation and recovery as reliability hardening.
rationale: Writing same-origin tab storage requires capability already sufficient for equivalent disruption.

source: Malformed same-version data in the user's own localStorage preference key.
closest_control: Preference loading accepts versioned data without runtime shape validation.
sink: Map location resolution synchronously dereferences the null value.
impact: The user loses local Map functionality until storage is cleared; no independent attacker boundary exists.
preconditions: Same-origin code, extension, local tool, or legacy corruption writes null.
User opens Map.
counterevidence: localStorage is same-origin.
An actor able to write it already controls the UI more directly.
Failure is local and recoverable.
proof_gaps: No shipped writer producing this exact value was identified.

evidence.claim: Versioned preference data is unchecked and dereferenced.
Location resolution invokes the loader synchronously.
Opening Map activates the path.

affected_locations.detail: A version match returns data without shape validation when isValid is omitted.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_parser

affected_locations.detail: loadPreferences accepts null, then synchronously dereferences stored.manualLocation; the same path is used by location resolution and preference updates.
affected_locations.label: missing_preferences_validator

affected_locations.detail: resolveLocation calls loadPreferences before it has returned a Promise, so the synchronous exception cannot become a rejection for the caller's .catch.
affected_locations.label: activation_path

affected_locations.detail: Opening Map invokes resolveLocation in an effect; no local try/catch or root error boundary contains a synchronous storage exception.
affected_locations.label: uncontained_effect_sink

fix_direction: Validate preference shape and reset malformed state as a correctness fix.
rationale: Same-origin storage poisoning adds no new security capability.

source: Untrusted model JSON on a prompt-only/schema-retry path with drug.productNameKo explicitly null.
closest_control: Fallback rejects null list elements but not required nested fields; grounding looks up null before AnswerValidator.
sink: Map.copyOf-backed groundedDrugs.get(null) throws and GlobalExceptionHandler returns HTTP 500.
impact: Malformed provider output suppresses an anonymous care-information answer after expensive upstream work.
preconditions: Schema enforcement is absent/retried away.
Model returns parseable drug object with null productNameKo.
Grounded map exists.
counterevidence: Schema-enforced provider paths should reject null.
Impact is availability-only and depends on malformed model output.
Valid names reach the validator normally.
proof_gaps: No live provider response with the exact null field was captured.

evidence.claim: Nested product identity is not checked.
Grounding performs nullable lookup before validation.
Schema-less retry and prompt-only paths exist.
Unhandled exception becomes internal error.

affected_locations.label: root_sink
affected_locations.detail: groundAllergyChecks calls Map.copyOf-backed map.get(null) before validator.

affected_locations.label: coercion_gap
affected_locations.detail: Rejects null list elements but not null nested productNameKo.

affected_locations.label: nested required productNameKo in provider schema
null key reaches immutable map before validator

fix_direction: Validate required nested fields before grounding and make lookup null-safe with a safe fail-closed answer.
rationale: Untrusted provider output deterministically erases care information before the intended validator.

source: A user opening any facility detail drawer while persistent disclaimer/emergency guidance is present.
closest_control: The fixed aria-modal overlay uses z-index 10001 while mandatory safety controls stay underneath.
sink: The opaque sheet/backdrop visually occludes disclaimer and cross-tab Call 119 guidance.
impact: Mandatory medical framing and emergency guidance become unavailable while facility information is read.
preconditions: Detail drawer is open.
The user needs the hidden disclaimer or active emergency banner.
counterevidence: Drawer has focus, Escape, backdrop, and close controls.
Safety state reappears after close.
Facility phone does not reproduce canonical guidance.
proof_gaps: No browser screenshot or assistive-technology run was performed.

evidence.claim: Full-viewport z-[10001] aria-modal covers shell.
Emergency banner and DisclaimerStrip remain outside overlay.
Canonical copy is intended to stay visible.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Fixed inset-0 z-50 aria-modal layer.

fix_direction: Render canonical disclaimer and active emergency control within/above every modal and browser-test it.
rationale: The UI routes around the always-visible disclaimer/emergency invariant.

source: Schema-less model JSON containing a non-null urgency object whose level is JSON null.
closest_control: Fallback fills only a wholly absent urgency; validator requires Call 119 only when level already equals EMERGENCY.
sink: Frontend preserves the object and gates emergency UI on exact 'emergency' equality.
impact: A real emergency missed by finite rule triage can render without authoritative state or persistent Call 119 guidance.
preconditions: EmergencyTriage misses the wording.
Model returns urgency.level null.
Response otherwise passes.
counterevidence: Rule triage catches enumerated patterns.
Unknown strings degrade upward to URGENT.
Provider schema requires valid level when enforced.
proof_gaps: No live model produced this explicit-null structure.

evidence.claim: Explicit null can leave nested level null while unknown strings map upward.
Nested level is unchecked and non-null urgency preserved.
Action invariant is one-way from already-EMERGENCY.
Persistent emergency state requires exact equality.

affected_locations.detail: Unknown urgency strings map upward to URGENT, but an explicit JSON null deserializes to null without invoking the enum creator.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Null-element checks omit urgency.level and withGuarantees replaces only a wholly null urgency object.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Pre-model triage is finite and its TODO identifies severe allergic reaction, seizure, poisoning/overdose, and head injury as uncovered cases.
affected_locations.label: closest_control

affected_locations.detail: The call-action invariant runs only when level equals EMERGENCY, so null incurs no violation.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Client normalization keeps a non-null urgency object even if its level member is null.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Emergency presentation is gated on level exactly equaling emergency, so null suppresses the banner.
affected_locations.label: sink
model prose rendered to the user
one_way_check
affected_locations.detail: The validator only requires an action after the model already chooses emergency.

affected_locations.label: pass_through
affected_locations.detail: Non-rule-path urgency is preserved from the model.

affected_locations.label: state_sink
affected_locations.detail: emergencyActive trusts latestAnswer.urgency.level.

affected_locations.label: render_sink
affected_locations.detail: The trusted flag controls a persistent cross-tab emergency banner.

fix_direction: Reject/fail upward on null level before grounding and validate emergency state independently.
rationale: A malformed model field can suppress emergency safety state outside finite pre-triage coverage.

source: A malformed government permission row with valid itemSeq but missing ITEM_NAME.
closest_control: Boundary mapping retains null names; sorting and grounding assume a non-null identity.
sink: DrugService comparator or immutable grounded map throws, and cached malformed data can repeat failures.
impact: One malformed provider record can suppress medicine information for affected public searches/chats.
preconditions: Provider/fixture/cache returns itemSeq with absent name.
Row reaches sorting or grounding.
Malformed row repeats or is cached.
counterevidence: Fixed TLS provider is not attacker-selected.
Some paths are null-safe.
Impact is availability, not false content.
proof_gaps: No actual malformed row was captured and cache persistence should be confirmed.

evidence.claim: Parser accepts null names when itemSeq exists.
Ingredient path uses null-unsafe name comparator.
Grounding passes name keys to Map.copyOf.

affected_locations.detail: List rows validate only itemSeq and detail mapping accepts a null ITEM_NAME into PermittedDetail.
affected_locations.label: missing boundary validation

affected_locations.detail: groundedDrugs inserts drug.nameKo as a map key without checking it and then calls Map.copyOf, which rejects null keys.
affected_locations.label: null-key root control

affected_locations.detail: The try/catch begins only around pass 2; a grounding exception from DrugContextRetriever escapes the chat answer path.
affected_locations.label: uncaught retrieval wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Permission detail results are cached by itemSeq, and a nonempty Optional containing a null name is eligible for caching.
affected_locations.label: persistent malformed-value window

affected_locations.detail: List rows are retained when itemSeq is non-null even if ITEM_NAME is absent; the malformed list is cacheable.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_parser

affected_locations.detail: The final comparator dereferences Drug.nameKo without null handling.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: Ingredient retrieval sorts untrusted rows before later filters/catches can contain the exception.
affected_locations.label: reachable_sink

fix_direction: Validate required provider fields before caching; drop malformed rows and contain failure per row.
rationale: Missing external-boundary validation can repeatedly remove safety-relevant care information.

source: Possession of a cryptographically random deviceId intentionally used as an anonymous bearer.
closest_control: The route authorizes solely by that bearer and stores it in same-origin localStorage.
sink: A holder can read/mutate the bearer profile.
impact: This demonstrates bearer semantics, not a guess, leak, or authorization bypass.
preconditions: An attacker independently obtains the victim's high-entropy deviceId.
counterevidence: Client uses crypto.randomUUID.
Candidate identifies no enumeration/disclosure path.
Possession-based authority is the explicit no-login design.
proof_gaps: Access-log/analytics retention of path identifiers was not assessed.

evidence.claim: Controller documents deviceId as anonymous bearer.
Client generates/retains identifier locally.
Lookup by supplied bearer is intentional.

fix_direction: Treat deviceId as secret, avoid logging, and rotate on suspected disclosure; add accounts only if threat model changes.
rationale: No independent disclosure, prediction, or authorization-bypass mechanism is shown.

source: Anonymous chat input that causes a schema-enforced provider request to return any HTTP 400.
closest_control: Retry predicate checks status only and assumes response_format incompatibility.
sink: A second authenticated provider request is sent without response_format but with attacker-driven content.
impact: Bad requests can double provider calls, latency, worker occupancy, and possibly metered input spend, while weakening shape guarantees.
preconditions: Model normally uses response_format.
Input causes non-schema HTTP 400.
Provider processes the retry.
counterevidence: Retry occurs once.
Some 400s are pre-billing.
Timeout/post-validation bound later behavior.
proof_gaps: Provider error bodies, billing, and a reproducible attacker-controlled non-schema 400 were not captured.

evidence.claim: Every schema-enforced HTTP 400 triggers schema-less retry.
Each attempt is a separate authenticated POST.

affected_locations.detail: The retry predicate checks only status 400 and assumes response_format caused it.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: post() performs each authenticated provider request.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The retry removes only response_format, retaining any attacker-driven cause such as oversized/invalid conversation input.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

fix_direction: Retry only on provider errors specifically identifying unsupported response_format.
rationale: Amplification is static but bounded to two calls; practical cost depends on provider behavior.

source: A normal open_now hospital search with more than 100 nearer eligible candidates.
closest_control: Service truncates distance-ranked candidates to 100 before fetching hours/open state.
sink: Only that prefix is filtered and returned, without an incompleteness signal.
impact: The service can report no open hospital while an in-radius open hospital exists after rank 100.
preconditions: More than 100 hospitals are in radius.
Needed open facility ranks after first 100.
Earlier candidates yield no returned open result.
counterevidence: Cap is deliberate and latency-measured.
First 100 nearest are checked.
Warm caches reduce later cost.
proof_gaps: No concrete live query demonstrated an open result beyond rank 100.

evidence.claim: Code explicitly acknowledges rank 101+ open hospitals stay invisible.
Truncation precedes detail/open filtering.

fix_direction: Parallelize/page details or return explicit partial/unknown rather than definitive empty results when truncated.
rationale: A documented cap can hide open care facilities during an ordinary request.

source: One HIRA detail call that fails during a configured live-mode nearby-hospital request.
closest_control: Hospital detail has no per-row degradation path, unlike the pharmacy timetable sibling.
sink: The mapper exception propagates through Parallel.map and fails the whole facility response.
impact: A partial provider failure becomes a whole-response 503 and hides otherwise valid nearby care locations.
preconditions: Multiple directory rows are valid.
One detail call throws rather than falls back.
Mode/config rethrows that failure.
counterevidence: HYBRID/fixture fallback avoids many exceptions.
Successful fan-out works normally.
Non-open-now directory rows could be retained with unknown hours.
proof_gaps: No live partial-failure integration reproduction was run.

evidence.claim: All hospital rows are collected through one fail-fast Parallel.map.
toHospital has no row-local catch/degradation.
Live-mode detail failure is rethrown.
Pharmacy sibling retains rows with unknown hours.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Parallel.map returns only after all detail mappings and propagates a mapper exception.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: toHospital calls findByYkiho without a per-row catch/degradation path.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The pharmacy sibling catches each optional schedule failure and retains the directory row, showing the intended degradation pattern.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: Live-mode detail failure is rethrown as PublicApiException.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Unlike pharmacy timetable lookup, hospital detail lookup has no per-row recovery.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Any mapped exception terminates collectList and the request.

fix_direction: Contain detail failures per row; retain directory facts with unknown hours, excluding only unconfirmed rows for open_now.
rationale: One optional upstream failure can suppress the entire nearby-care result.

source: Malformed/compromised Naver geocode JSON containing non-finite or out-of-range coordinates.
closest_control: Double.parseDouble is followed by no finite or geographic range validation.
sink: GeocodeController returns the values as a successful result to the browser.
impact: Invalid coordinates can break or misplace map and nearby-care guidance while the API reports success.
preconditions: Fixed TLS upstream returns parseable invalid x/y.
Client consumes the result.
counterevidence: Naver is fixed and TLS-authenticated.
User normally selects a result.
Later facility validation rejects some invalid values.
proof_gaps: Jackson non-finite serialization and frontend behavior were not browser-tested.
No real invalid provider response was captured.

evidence.claim: Coordinates are parsed and returned without finite/range checks.
Parsed results are emitted as success.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: parseDouble accepts `NaN` and infinities and no latitude/longitude range or finite check follows.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The values are serialized to the browser as a successful geocode result.

fix_direction: Reject non-finite values and enforce longitude/latitude ranges at the provider boundary.
rationale: Unchecked provider geometry can corrupt a safety-relevant location flow; fixed-upstream dependence limits confidence.

source: A drug assembled from permission, e약은요, and four DUR operation families.
closest_control: Drug carries one SourceRef and discards per-component dataset identity/retrieval origin.
sink: The UI shows one source titled MFDS drug product licence information beneath narrative and DUR facts.
impact: Users and validators cannot identify which dataset supplied a safety fact, when it was retrieved, or whether that component fell back.
preconditions: Detailed drug includes narrative or DUR facts.
Composite card is returned/rendered.
counterevidence: All components share MFDS agency and ITEM_SEQ on successful live calls.
Some warning prose names MFDS DUR.
Wrong fixture/live origin is separately covered by R01-CAN-120.

evidence.claim: Record combines three datasets but exposes one source.
Join assigns only a permission-dataset SourceRef.
Sole source chip/title appears beneath composite card.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The service joins permission, easy-drug narrative, and four DUR warning results into one Drug but assigns only source(itemSeq).

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The sole SourceRef title names only MFDS drug product licence information.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The Drug record explicitly contains facts from three separate datasets but has a single source field.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: DUR safety warnings are fetched from distinct operation families and lose operation-level provenance at the join.

fix_direction: Use per-component provenance or a composite source enumerating operations, times, and actual origins.
rationale: The residual defect is operation-level source-title and retrieval-granularity loss; successful live components share MFDS and ITEM_SEQ, while wrong binding and fixture origin are separate P0 findings, so this does not meet the repository's narrow P1 care-delivery threshold.

source: Public query strings containing many comma-separated exclude_ingredients values.
closest_control: Spring binds an unconstrained Set and normalizes all entries before nested ingredient comparisons.
sink: AllergyChecker work scales across both ingredients and avoided keys for each returned product.
impact: Large accepted exclusion sets may increase CPU/allocation per public lookup under repetition.
preconditions: Server accepts a sufficiently large request line.
Upstream returns enough ingredients/products.
Requests repeat concurrently.
counterevidence: HTTP request-line limits impose an effective bound.
Upstream result shapes are bounded.
No benchmark shows material amplification.
proof_gaps: Measure maximum accepted exclusion cardinality and concurrent worst-case CPU before classification.

evidence.claim: Public endpoints bind/normalize exclusions without explicit count/length limits.
Comparison is nested across ingredients and avoided keys.

affected_locations.detail: Both unauthenticated endpoints bind an unconstrained Set of raw exclusions.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: Every supplied value is normalized and retained without a collection/element limit.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Nested ingredient-by-avoid-key comparison makes cost proportional to both dimensions.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Measure first, then enforce a small explicit count/element length if workload is material.
rationale: Unbounded code shape is real, but transport caps and unmeasured amplification prevent confident reporting.

source: A HIRA detail row whose nonempty lunchWeek uses an unrecognized/malformed interval.
closest_control: Strict parsing collapses malformed and absent lunch into Optional.empty.
sink: FacilityService treats empty as no lunch closure and may return OPEN from weekday hours.
impact: A user can be directed to a hospital actually closed for lunch.
preconditions: Provider returns meaningful closure in unsupported format.
Current time is inside actual lunch and broad weekday hours.
Facility is returned.
counterevidence: Captured official fixture matches accepted format.
UI encourages calling ahead.
No live variant was reproduced.
proof_gaps: Verify live HIRA formats and reproduce malformed-but-meaningful data through FacilityService.

evidence.claim: Nonmatching/invalid/backwards lunch becomes Optional.empty.
Empty lunch bypasses closure and permits OPEN.

affected_locations.detail: Any lunchWeek text outside one strict tilde pattern becomes Optional.empty.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Optional.empty bypasses lunch closure and allows an OPEN result from broad weekday hours.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Distinguish absent, valid, and malformed lunch; malformed nonempty data must produce hours unknown.
rationale: The parser fails open on a care schedule field, with provider-format uncertainty retained.

source: A nested .env or .env.* file that is force-staged or already tracked.
closest_control: Pre-commit filename matching covers only root .env names and relies on incomplete content heuristics otherwise.
sink: A nested environment file whose content evades regexes can enter a public commit.
impact: A live credential can be committed to the public repository.
preconditions: Nested environment file is staged despite ignore.
Content evades limited regexes.
Hook is active and commit proceeds.
counterevidence: .gitignore blocks ordinary staging at any depth.
Content scanner and CI gitleaks are additional layers.
No real secret was staged.
proof_gaps: Remote CI/branch protection enforcement was not tested.

evidence.claim: Filename case omits */.env and */.env.*.
Only limited content patterns backstop filename miss.
Ordinary staging ignores environment files but force-add/prior tracking remain.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: All newly staged paths are enumerated.

affected_locations.label: broken-control
affected_locations.detail: The case pattern matches .env only at repository root and does not include */.env or */.env.*.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: A nested .env containing a credential that evades the limited content patterns can be committed.

fix_direction: Match environment files at every depth, leaving only .env.example excepted.
rationale: Deterministic filename bypass can route around the never-commit-.env invariant.

source: An added line containing a real credential and a broad placeholder word such as example.
closest_control: After a secret regex matches, grep -Ev suppresses the whole line based on any placeholder word.
sink: The hook sees no remaining hit and permits the credential-bearing commit.
impact: A live secret can enter the public repository despite the last-line guard.
preconditions: Line matches configured secret regex.
Same line contains a placeholder token.
No later local control blocks commit.
counterevidence: Line must first match a configured pattern.
CI gitleaks may reject later push.
No secret/commit was created.
proof_gaps: Remote CI/branch protection enforcement was not observed.

evidence.claim: Whole matching lines are discarded when any broad placeholder token appears.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Added staged lines are scanned for credential-shaped values.

affected_locations.label: broken-control
affected_locations.detail: Whole matching lines are dropped when they contain broad words such as example or change-me, independent of which token is the credential.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: A real credential on a comment or assignment line containing an allowlisted word reaches the commit.

fix_direction: Classify only the matched credential value against exact placeholders; never suppress the whole line.
rationale: A simple deterministic line composition bypasses the secret guard.

source: Dependency installation in a detected Git checkout where local hooksPath configuration fails.
closest_control: Installer converts in-checkout git-config failure to warnings and successful exit.
sink: Later commits run without .githooks/pre-commit when the warning is missed.
impact: The secret guard can be absent while setup appears successful, increasing risk of credential commits and later publication.
preconditions: Checkout is detected.
Local git config write fails.
User continues and commits sensitive content.
Other scanners miss it.
counterevidence: Recommended bin/setup.sh independently configures hooks fail-closed.
Warnings are explicit.
CI scans later.
Non-checkout installs intentionally must succeed.
proof_gaps: Prevalence of config failures and warning visibility are unmeasured.

evidence.claim: In-checkout configuration failure is warned but exits successfully.
Guard depends on core.hooksPath.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: The prepare script attempts to configure core.hooksPath.

affected_locations.label: broken-control
affected_locations.detail: Any git-config failure is converted to warnings and a successful process exit.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Subsequent commits execute without the repository secret guard when hooksPath remains unset.

fix_direction: Distinguish non-checkout skip from in-checkout failure; make the latter fail or emit machine-checkable failure.
rationale: The control is disabled only after a local config failure and missed warning, below direct P0 bypasses.

source: The same nonblank model-authored disclaimer field already evaluated by Batch 02 R01-CAN-054.
closest_control: The target UI ignores answer.disclaimer and renders an unconditional code-authored DisclaimerStrip.
sink: No target caller renders the model-authored field; the claimed direct or future API consumer is hypothetical.
impact: No demonstrated target impact remains beyond API contract hygiene.
preconditions: A consumer not present in the target would have to render answer.disclaimer as its safety control.
counterevidence: MobileShell always renders the code-authored DisclaimerStrip.
Batch 02 R01-CAN-054 validated the same deployed field path as not reportable.

evidence.claim: Schema-less retry and prompt-only paths exist.
Any nonblank model disclaimer is retained.
Validator lacks canonical equality check.
Grounding preserves and envelope serializes it.
First-party fixed copy is an independent mitigation.

affected_locations.label: reachable_schema_fallback
affected_locations.detail: A provider 400 causes an intentional retry without response_format.

affected_locations.label: reachable_prompt_only_mode
affected_locations.detail: Models outside the allowlist run with prompt-only structured output.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: withGuarantees substitutes the server disclaimer only for null/blank values and otherwise retains arbitrary model text.

affected_locations.label: pass_through
affected_locations.detail: ground() copies answer.disclaimer unchanged.

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: The disclaimer is rejected only if it contains the markup regex; equality with the server-owned mandatory notice is never checked.

affected_locations.label: response_sink
affected_locations.detail: The retained disclaimer is emitted to the client.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Model JSON is deserialized into the answer.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Canonical copy is used only for blank/null model values.

affected_locations.label: incomplete_control_1
affected_locations.detail: The disclaimer receives markup scanning, not canonical ownership validation.

affected_locations.label: incomplete_control_2
affected_locations.detail: The disclaimer receives markup scanning, not canonical ownership validation.

affected_locations.label: pass_through
affected_locations.detail: Grounding preserves answer.disclaimer.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The answer is serialized into the response envelope.

affected_locations.label: contract
affected_locations.detail: The client contract says the field is always present and should be rendered.

affected_locations.label: mitigation
affected_locations.detail: The first-party shell independently displays canonical copy.

fix_direction: No security fix for this candidate; preserve the target's immutable visible disclaimer control.
rationale: This is remediation-equivalent to R01-CAN-054 and adds only a speculative non-target consumer, not a reachable target sink.
